case_title: KOLBY TATE MONROE VS. PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES, ET AL

docket_entries.description: DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE TURNER, PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF SAN FRANCISCO PROBATION DEPT. RECORDS OF DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THIS CASE INVOLVES A THIRD PARTY SUBPOENA DIRECTED TO THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO TO OBTAIN PROBATION REPORTS. THE CITY'S POSITION IS THAT A COURT ORDER IS REQUIRED TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO THE RECORDS ON A SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE. THE CITY EXPRESSES NO OBJECTION TO PRODUCING THE RECORDS, BUT TAKES NO POSITION ON WHETHER GOOD CAUSE HAS BEEN SHOWN. THE UNDERSIGNED AGREES WITH THE MOVING PARTY THAT THERE IS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PRODUCTION OF THE SAN FRANCISCO ADULT PROBATION DEPARTMENT ("SFAPD") RECORDS. THE UNDERSIGNED WOULD ORDER THE SFAPD TO COMPLY WITH THE SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS CONCERNING RECORDS FOR DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ WITHIN 15 DAYS. DEFENSE COUNSEL, ROBERT MARCHI TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM IN WORD FORMAT DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT TIM@NCALEGAL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: J. TIMOTHY NARDELL, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 66488899) FILED BY DEFENDANT TURNER, GEORGE , AN INDIVIDUAL PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, APR-30-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS GEORGE TURNER ANDD PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER STIPULATION. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS GEORGE TURNER AND PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' "MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES PER STIPULATION" IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT, READ AS WHOLE, ADEQUATELY PLED GROUNDS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PARAGRAPH 53 RE-ALLEGED AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE ALL THE COMPLAINT'S ALLEGATIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel The Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Kolby Monroe
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM videoconference:
Aryan Behnamjou, Esq., Tel. 415.464.8888, for the Plaintiff;
Robert Marchi, Esq., Tel. 415.464.8888, for the Defendants.

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The parties and the judge pro tem (appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation on their behalf.

Matter called. Argument/discussions on DEFENDANTS GEORGE TURNER, PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' Motion To Compel The Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Kolby Monroe are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the claim of $1,000,000.00 in the statement of damages is withdrawn. The Court states that the PTSD claim is withdrawn, and the request for sanctions is denied.

Mr. Marchi is to prepare the proposed order and submit it to plaintiff's counsel. The proposed order is to be submitted via email in Word format to Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: J. Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Per Stipulation
Off Calendar Apr-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ARYAN BEHNAMJOU, Esq. of West Coast Trial Lawyers, APLC (Tel: 213-927-3700) for plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT W. MARCHI, Esq. of Bradley, Curley, Barrabee & Kowalski, P.C. (Tel: 415-464-8888) for defendants Phatt Chance Community Services and George Turner.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GEORGE TURNER ANDD PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Per Stipulation.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants George Turner and Phatt Chance Community Services' "motion to strike punitive damages per stipulation" is denied. The complaint, read as whole, adequately pled grounds for punitive damages. Paragraph 53 re-alleged and incorporated by reference all the complaint's allegations.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of San Francisco Probation Dept. Records Of Defendant Salvador Chavez
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Robert Marchi, Esq. 415-464-8888 for Defendant.
Kaitlyn Murphy, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3867 for non-party.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GEORGE TURNER, PHATT CHANCE COMMUNITY SERVICES' Motion To Compel Production Of San Francisco Probation Dept. Records Of Defendant Salvador Chavez.

The motion is granted. This case involves a third party subpoena directed to the City and County of San Francisco to obtain probation reports. The City's position is that a court order is required to provide access to the records on a showing of good cause. The City expresses no objection to producing the records, but takes no position on whether good cause has been shown. The undersigned agrees with the moving party that there is good cause for the production of the San Francisco Adult Probation Department ("SFAPD") records. The undersigned would order the SFAPD to comply with the Subpoena for Business Records concerning records for Defendant Salvador Chavez within 15 days.

Defense Counsel, Robert Marchi to submit a proposed order approved as to form in word format directly to the Judge pro tem at tim@ncalegal.com.

Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
